Title: Police bust crypto scam call centre in Batu Ferringhi, 5 foreigners nabbed
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police have busted a cryptocurrency investment scam call centre targeting Chinese nationals in a raid at Batu Ferringhi, arresting five foreigners.
State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the operation by the Northeast District Police Commercial Crime Investigation Department yesterday led to the raid on premises in Batu Ferringhi.
He said five foreigners – three men and two women aged between 24 and 46 – were detained for allegedly running an online call centre linked to fictitious USDT cryptocurrency investment and exchange scams.
Among the items seized were 10 mobile phones, four laptops, a CPU unit, two keyboards, three computer mice, a router and a monitor, believed to have been used in the syndicate's operations.
The case is being investigated under Sections 420 and 120B of the Penal Code for cheating and criminal conspiracy, which provide for imprisonment, whipping and fines upon conviction.
Azizee said all suspects had been remanded to assist investigations.
He said the success of the operation was due to public tip-offs and close cooperation, which enabled the Commercial Crime Investigation Department to conduct surveillance and dismantle the international syndicate.
He added that police would continue to intensify operations to combat cybercrime and cross-border scam syndicates using the country as their base.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hyundai Malaysia launches Ioniq 5 N, Ioniq 6 N high-performance EVs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hyundai Motor Malaysia has launched the high-performance electric vehicles (EVs) Ionic 5 N and Ionic 6 N, with prices starting from RM443,888 and RM449,888 respectively.
The launch, held at the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show (KLIMS) 2026, marks the introduction of Hyundai's N performance EV line-up to the Malaysian market for the first time.
Managing director Jahabarnisa Haja Mohideen said the two models demonstrate that electric vehicles can deliver both performance and driving excitement without compromising everyday usability.
"Malaysians have always had a passion for driving. Here at KLIMS 2026, we are proud to finally give that passion something truly worthy of it.
"The Hyundai Ionic 5 N and Ionic 6 N are testaments that electric performance vehicles can be equally thrilling and more. When customers get behind the wheel, they will know you never just drive with Hyundai N. You enjoy every second of it," she said.
The Ionic 5 N, Hyundai's high-performance electric crossover is powered by a drivetrain capable of producing up to 650PS with N grin boost activated.
It accelerates from 0-100km/h in 3.4 seconds and has a top speed of 260km/h.
The model also offers a WLTP driving range of up to 448km and features Hyundai's N-exclusive technologies, including N e-Shift, N active sound+, N drift optimiser, N torque distribution and N launch control.
Meanwhile, the Ionic 6 N performance sedan delivers the same 650PS output and completes the 0-100km/h sprint in 3.2 seconds, with a top speed of 257km/h.
Built on the aerodynamic Ionic 6 platform, the model offers a longer WLTP range of up to 487km and comes equipped with a stroke-sensing electronically controlled suspension system for enhanced driving dynamics.
It said both vehicles embody the brand's "Never Just Drive" philosophy, which is built around three core pillars: cornering performance, everyday usability and race-track capability.
Hyundai N's performance engineering is derived from its experience in the world Rally Championship with all N models developed at Hyundai's Namyang Research and Development Centre in South Korea and validated at Germany's Nürburgring Nordschleife circuit.
The Ionic 5 N and Ionic 6 N are available on a built-to-order basis in Malaysia.
Bookings can be made through Hyundai N authorised dealers namely Sime Darby Auto Hyundai Sdn Bhd in Kuala Lumpur and Selangor, Goh Brothers Motor Sdn Bhd in Penang and Heng Lian Enterprise Bhd in Kuching.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia eyes RM6.1bil oil and gas services opportunities from Middle East rebuild
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is eyeing up to US$1.5 billion (about RM6.1 billion) in potential procurement opportunities for local oil and gas services and equipment (OGSE) companies from the rebuilding of energy infrastructure in the Middle East.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the rebuilding and repair of oil, gas and petrochemical infrastructure in the region was expected to involve high costs, estimated between US$42 billion (RM170 billion) and US$57 billion (RM231 billion).
He said the crisis had created a need to repair and rebuild affected facilities, with initial estimates covering 158 production fields and 21 processing plants.
Akmal said this presented realistic opportunities for Malaysian OGSE companies.
"This has the potential to open realistic procurement opportunities, which are quite conservative, worth about US$1.5 billion for Malaysian companies.
"For the government, the crisis should not only be managed from the perspective of risk, but also viewed from the perspective of opportunities to strengthen the competitiveness of the country's industries," he said during a livestreamed briefing on the energy crisis today.
He said the government was prepared to provide diplomatic support to facilitate access for Malaysian OGSE companies to major firms and contractors in the region.
Akmal said the Malaysia Petroleum Resources Corporation (MPRC) should continue to play its role as the lead agency for the OGSE sector, particularly in building capacity, expanding market access and translating overseas opportunities into business returns.
He said Malaysia already had strong fundamentals in the OGSE industry, which recorded revenue of RM94.5 billion for the 2024 financial year.
The sector also recorded RM23 billion in export revenue and comprised 3,765 local companies, supporting more than 123,000 jobs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GWM Malaysia unveils new hybrid SUVs at KLIMS 2026
Article:
GWM Malaysia has previewed two new electrified models - the Ora 5 HEV and Haval H7 Hi4 PHEV - at the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show (KLIMS) 2026, as the automaker expands its hybrid and plug-in hybrid offerings.
The models form part of GWM Malaysia's broader strategy to position its Ora and Haval brands with distinct roles, targeting different customer needs ranging from urban mobility to SUV capability.
Cui Anqi, managing director of corporate affairs at GWM Malaysia, said Malaysia's mobility market is becoming more diverse as consumers make vehicle choices based on lifestyle and usage requirements.
"Malaysia's mobility landscape is becoming increasingly diverse, with customers choosing vehicles based on lifestyle, purpose and personal priorities.
"The Ors 5 HEV and Haval H7 Hi4 PHEV embody this shift, strengthening how we position each brand within the GWM ecosystem while advancing a clearer, more intentional approach to electrified mobility for the Malaysian market," she said.
ORA Expands Into Hybrid Segment
The Ora 5 HEV is positioned as a compact crossover aimed at urban users, powered by GWM's new Hi2 intelligent hybrid system.
The model produces up to 223 PS and 476 Nm of torque, with a claimed 0-100 km/h acceleration time of 7.7 seconds and fuel consumption of 4.4 litres per 100km.
With a combined driving range exceeding 1,100km, GWM said the Ora 5 HEV is designed for both city commuting and longer-distance travel.
The model measures 4,471mm long, 1,833mm wide and 1,641mm tall, with a 2,720mm wheelbase.
GWM said the Ora 5 also received the "Design of the Year" title at the 2025 London Design Awards and achieved a Guinness World Records title for lowest fuel consumption by a production hybrid SUV driving across municipalities in China.
HAVAL Targets Electrified SUV Market
The Haval H7 Hi4 PHEV, meanwhile, expands the company's electrified SUV range with a plug-in hybrid system delivering 370 PS and 750 Nm of torque.
It can accelerate from 0-100 km/h in 6.2 seconds, while offering up to 150km of pure electric range and a combined range exceeding 1,000km.
Measuring 4,800mm long, 1,950mm wide and 1,843mm tall, the SUV is aimed at customers seeking a larger vehicle with off-road styling and hybrid efficiency.
Roslan Abdullah, chief operating officer of GWM Malaysia, said the two models reflect the company's direction in Malaysia.
"What makes these previews important is not just the products themselves; they reflect the direction of our portfolio in Malaysia.
"The Ora 5 HEV represents connected and lifestyle-led mobility, while the Haval H7 Hi4 PHEV expands our SUV proposition into an electrified and versatile space," he said.
The GWM showcase at KLIMS 2026 also features models including the Haval H6 HEV, WEY G9 Hi4 PHEV, Tank 300 HEV, Tank 500 HEV and Ora Good CAT EV.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Activist warns Rohingya issue could affect Malaysia's global image
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Rohingya issue can no longer be viewed solely through a humanitarian lens, but must also take into account national interests, social security and the perception of foreign visitors, a community activist said.
Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) public relations chief Daniel Khoo said a clearer and more comprehensive government plan is needed to manage issues involving the Rohingya community in Malaysia, in order to safeguard the country's image as a tourism and investment destination.
He said public concern over the issue was growing, particularly due to the presence of Rohingya individuals, including children, seeking donations and begging in tourist hotspots such as the area around the Petronas Twin Towers, Bukit Bintang and other key locations.
"Malaysia is among the countries that depend heavily on the tourism sector. What tourists see when they arrive here will shape their perception of Malaysia.
"When major tourist areas are filled with individuals soliciting donations or assistance by the roadside, it will naturally raise questions among foreign visitors about the actual situation in the country," he said.
Khoo stressed that the issue should not be used as a reason to incite hatred against any community, but instead serve as a catalyst for the government to seek more effective solutions.
He said the government should strengthen cooperation with the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees and third countries to find a long-term solution to the Rohingya refugee issue.
"Malaysia has played a significant humanitarian role for many years. However, after such a long period, it is time for a more sustainable solution to be established so that the issue does not continue indefinitely.
"We do not want the situation to become increasingly complicated to the point of creating social tensions or negative perceptions of the country," he said.
According to Khoo, issues that require immediate attention include children living on the streets, access to basic education and monitoring activities that could affect public order.
He added that the government must also ensure major tourist destinations remain orderly and comfortable, as these locations are often the first impression foreign visitors have of Malaysia.
"When tourists come to Malaysia, they want to see a country that is modern, organised and safe. Any prolonged social issues in major tourist areas will undoubtedly affect that perception.
"This is not about blaming any party but about how we manage existing challenges so they do not damage the country's reputation," he said.
Khoo said the government should act more proactively before the issue escalates further, noting that public sentiment towards the Rohingya community had become increasingly evident through online discussions and petitions circulating on social media.
"What is important now is finding a solution. We need to protect the country's image, safeguard the interests of Malaysians and at the same time ensure humanitarian considerations continue to be addressed in a balanced manner," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Capital A says no financial hit from Singapore share seizure
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Capital A Bhd said it does not expect any financial impact or losses arising from a notice of seizure issued by the sheriff of the Supreme Court of Singapore.
The seizure involves shares in Teleport Everywhere Pte Ltd and BigPay Pte Ltd held through Move Digital Sdn Bhd.
In a Bursa Malaysia reply to query, the group said the notice covers only Move Digital's direct shareholdings, comprising 11.45 per cent in Teleport Everywhere and 99.56 per cent in BigPay.
Capital A said it owns 76.86 per cent of Teleport Everywhere through a combination of its direct stake of 65.41 per cent and Move Digital's 11.45 per cent stake, while it holds 99.56 per cent of BigPay through Move Digital.
It added that Teleport Everywhere and BigPay are not major subsidiaries, as each contributed less than 70 per cent of Capital A's pre-tax profit or total assets on a consolidated basis for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025.
Capital A said the US$14.7 million buyout sum under the partial arbitration award has already been accounted for in its audited financial statements for the year ended Dec 31, 2024.
Pending the outcome of the objection, the notice is not expected to have any financial impact on the group and will not interfere with ongoing operations.
As at Dec 31, 2025, the total cost of investment in Teleport Everywhere was RM11.5 million, while BigPay was RM287.3 million.
The net book value was negative US$21 million (RM85.1 million) for Teleport Everywhere and negative RM502 million for BigPay.
Move Digital will file a notice of objection with the Sheriff of the Supreme Court of Singapore within 14 days of receiving the notice, by June 23.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four nabbed in Butterworth call centre scam targeting Singaporeans
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: Police have dismantled a call centre-linked investment scam syndicate targeting Singaporeans in a raid here, arresting four men.
The operation yesterday was conducted following intelligence from a special operation involving the Bukit Aman Commercial Criminal Investigation Department (CCID) and its Seberang Prai Utara counterpart, at a premises in Butterworth.
State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the premises was being used as a call centre to promote non-existent investment schemes targeting victims in Singapore through social media platform "Tantan."
"Four men, aged between 20 and 36, were detained for their suspected involvement in running the online scam operation.
"We also seized five mobile phones, three laptops, a modem, an access card and a house key, believed to have been used in the fraudulent activities," he said in a statement.
The case is being investigated under Sections 420 and 120B of the Penal Code for cheating and criminal conspiracy, which carry penalties of imprisonment of between one and 10 years, whipping and fines upon conviction.
Azizee said all suspects have been remanded to assist investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi: Na'imah not Bersama's 'moneybags', I am main funder
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersama de facto leader Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations that the party received funds linked to the late former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin's widow.
He said Toh Puan Na'imah Abdul Khalid was not the party's financier, adding that he remained Bersama's largest funder at present.
During a Facebook Live session today, Rafizi said he personally paid RM30,000 for the party's launch last month and an additional RM50,000 deposit for its headquarters in Puchong.
He said remaining operational costs were currently being financed through loans and instalment arrangements as the party builds its financial base.
Bersama has raised about RM750,000 through lifetime membership fees, merchandise sales and public crowdfunding involving more than 27,000 Malaysians.
"These include members who paid RM20 lifetime fees, over 1,100 merchandise buyers and about 2,000 crowdfunding contributors," he said.
"Bersama deliberately avoids reliance on any single large donor to avoid political dependency risks.
"All of you are Bersama's funders. It is difficult to depend on small RM10 and RM50 contributions, but we do not want Bersama to be controlled by any funder," he said.
Rafizi also announced that the party would hold a fundraising dinner on June 27 at SJKC Wangsa Maju, with ticket prices ranging from RM150 to RM2,000 per person and tables priced up to RM18,000.
He said proceeds would be used to fund campaign vehicles for upcoming state elections in Johor and Negri Sembilan, party operations, office renovations and campaign materials ahead of the 16th General Election.
The allegations over party funding are believed to stem from unverified claims circulating on social media involving NGO financing and alleged links to political figures, which have been strongly denied by the organisations involved.
Bersama had previously
rejected claims
of an RM8.6 million takeover involving its leadership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kerjaya Prospek, RAC ink Prai land development pact
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd has signed a development agreement through its joint venture Rivanis Ventures Sdn Bhd with Railway Assets Corp (RAC) to buy and develop a 14.1-hectare plot in Prai, Penang.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the group said the mixed development project will be carried out over an estimated seven-year period and will comprise affordable housing, residential and commercial components.
The acquisition and development is estimated at RM156.49 million, comprising a cash payment of RM54.1 million and 338 affordable housing units valued at RM102.39 million.
Under the agreement, Rivanis will build at least 338 affordable housing units under the Rumah Mampu Milik Category C3 scheme, relocate existing occupants on the land into the housing units and develop additional residential and commercial properties on the site.
The development land is in Prai, Seberang Perai Tengah, Penang, near key commercial and residential hubs including Butterworth, Bukit Mertajam and Penang Island.
Kerjaya Prospek said the area is expected to benefit from infrastructure developments such as the Penang Sentral transport hub, the Mutiara Light Rail Transit line and the upcoming Juru-Sungai Dua Elevated Highway.
Funding for the project will come from a combination of shareholders' capital contributions to Rivanis and external borrowings, with the final funding mix yet to be determined.
The proposed acquisition and development carry a highest percentage ratio of about 13 per cent under Bursa Malaysia's listing requirements.
Kerjaya Prospek said the agreement is subject to several conditions' precedent, including planning approvals and shareholder approvals at the joint venture level.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two Malaysian peacekeepers wounded in airstrike in Lebanon
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two Malaysian peacekeepers were lightly wounded in an airstrike in Lebanon today.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the two members of the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 were part of a logistics convoy about 1km west of the United Nations base in Tibnin when an airstrike occurred.
The incident, about 9.25am local time (4.25am Malaysia), saw the duo, on duty as part of the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil), being hit by pieces of glass from the vehicle in which they were travelling.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Weststar to supply 300 electric vans to Yinson GreenTech
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Weststar Group, through its automotive arm Weststar Maxus Sdn Bhd, has signed an agreement with Yinson GreenTech to supply 300 electric commercial vans.
The collaboration is aimed at expanding the adoption of commercial electric vehicles (EV) by providing businesses with sustainable and efficient transportation solutions.
Weststar group managing director Tan Sri Dr Syed Azman Syed Ibrahim said sustainability has become a business imperative, particularly in the commercial transportation sector.
"This collaboration with Yinson GreenTech reflects our long-term commitment to supporting Malaysia's transition towards a lower-carbon economy while enabling businesses to adopt practical and scalable electric mobility solutions," he said.
The agreement was signed at the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show (KLIMS) 2026 and witnessed by Deputy Prime Minister II and Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.
Representing the parties were Weststar automotive division chief operating officer Julius Jonathan Netto and Yinson GreenTech managing director of EV charging and leasing Kelvin Soon.
Also present were SAIC Maxus president Yang Haijing and Yinson Holdings Bhd group executive chairman Lim Han Weng.
Weststar has already deployed hundreds of Maxus eDeliver 3 and eDeliver 5 electric vans to logistics operators nationwide.
Following the signing ceremony, Weststar Maxus unveiled its latest vehicle line-up at KLIMS 2026, strengthening its presence across both EV and internal combustion engine (ICE) segments.
Among the highlights was the Maxus eDeliver 5 electric panel van, designed for logistics operations and equipped with advanced driver assistance systems such as Adaptive Cruise Control, Autonomous Emergency Braking and Lane Keeping Assist.
The company also showcased the facelifted Maxus T60 (RM85,888) pickup truck, powered by a two-litre turbo diesel engine producing 120 kilowatts and 375 Newton metres (Nm) of torque, as well as the all-new Maxus Terron 9 premium pickup (RM140,888) featuring a 2.5-litre turbo diesel engine delivering up to 520Nm of torque and advanced off-road capabilities.
Also making its debut was the all-new Maxus eTerron 9 electric pickup (RM223,888), equipped with a 102.2 kilowatt-hour battery, dual-motor all-wheel-drive system and a driving range of up to 430 kilometres under the Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure standard.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High Court acquits neurologist of outraging modesty, finds 'serious prejudice' in trial
Article:
KUANTAN: The High Court has acquitted a neurologist who was sentenced to one year's jail for allegedly using criminal force to outrage the modesty of a female private hospital staff twice five years ago.
Judicial Commissioner Samry Masri ruled that the amendment of the original charge from Section 377D of the Penal Code for outraging decency to Section 354 which was amended at the end of the prosecution's case had caused serious prejudice to the doctor's defence and rendered the conviction unsafe.
He allowed the doctor's appeal on the conviction and sentence against a magistrate's court ruling in May last year.
The prosecution alleged that the incident took place in a hospital corridor and later inside a lift while the complainant was on duty.
However, at the end of the prosecution's case, the trial court found that the original charge could not be established and amended it to a charge under Section 354 before calling for defence and convicted him.
Samry held that the amendment fundamentally altered the nature of the charge and caused actual and serious prejudice to the appellant.
"The amendment is not a small change but a transformation of the nature and character of the charge itself," he said in a written judgment dated June 8.
The court noted that by the time the prosecution had closed its case, the defence strategy had already been shaped around the elements of the original Section 377D charge.
He further held that the prejudice suffered by the appellant could not be cured simply by allowing witnesses to be recalled.
Samry also found that the trial court did not show that the appellant had been meaningfully informed of his rights following the amendment, including his right to recall witnesses, conduct further cross-examination and present additional evidence and affected his right to a fair trial.
Apart from the procedural irregularity, the judge found that the conviction itself was unsafe based on the evidence presented at trial.
The court identified several material weaknesses in the prosecution's case, including contradictions in the complainant's account of how the incident occurred, the position of the parties, the alleged touching and her conduct immediately afterwards.
Among the inconsistencies highlighted was a discrepancy between the complainant's police report and her court testimony regarding who entered the lift first.
In her police report, she said that she entered the lift and was followed by the doctor.
However, during trial she testified that the appellant entered first, and acknowledged that her evidence conflicted with both the police report and CCTV footage.
The court held that these were not minor discrepancies but contradictions affecting the entire sequence of events.
Samry also questioned the complainant's conduct after the alleged incident, noting that she entered the same lift as the appellant immediately afterwards without showing any apparent attempt to avoid him.
"If it is true that she had just become a victim of sexual assault, the question is how she could continue to follow the appellant into the same lift.
"The prosecution had failed to conclusively establish the element of criminal force, which is central to an offence under Section 354," he said.
He said while the complainant alleged that her breast had been touched, the CCTV footage did not clearly show such an act.
Samry found that the appellant's defence had remained consistent throughout the proceedings.
"The doctor maintained that he had merely pointed at words printed on the complainant's clothing and had no intention of outraging her modesty," he said.
He further held that the trial court had been too quick to dismiss the defence as an afterthought, particularly given that the original charge had only been amended after the close of the prosecution's case and the conviction could not be regarded as safe.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt watching 'albino light' trend amid safety concerns - Loke
Article:
NILAI: Vehicle owners have been reminded that the installation of 'lampu albino', a term commonly used to describe high-intensity white aftermarket LED lighting systems, has not received approval from the Road Transport Department (RTD).
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said enforcement action could be taken if the lights are found to endanger other road users.
He said the installation of such lighting, increasingly seen on lorries and other vehicles on highways, is being carried out without any technical approval from the RTD.
"The government takes seriously the trend of installing these lights and is studying their impact before making any further decision on their use.
"Currently, no technical approval has been given for such lighting systems. They are installed voluntarily by vehicle owners.
"Action will be taken if they are found to be disruptive or affect the safety of other road users," he told reporters after officiating the opening of the RTD Nilai branch office here today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit closes slightly higher against US dollar, major currencies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended slightly higher against the greenback and other major currencies on Thursday, shrugging off the impact of stronger-than-expected US inflation data for May.
At 6pm, the local note inched up to 4.0650/0690 versus the greenback from Wednesday's close of 4.0670/0715.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit held steady at the RM4.06 level despite the US consumer price index (CPI) hitting a three-year high in May at 4.2 per cent. "This has happened ahead of the US Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting next week on June 16 and 17.
The US Federal Reserve (Fed) is likely to maintain its restrictive monetary policy stance next week, which can be US dollar-positive in the near term," he told Bernama.
At the same time, Mohd Afzanizam said uncertainties over the war in Iran will keep crude oil prices elevated, keeping the inflation rate high.
The local currency traded higher against a basket of major currencies.
It climbed versus the Japanese yen to 2.5321/5347 from 2.5347/5377 at Wednesday's close, gained against the British pound to 5.4325/4378 from 5.4486/4546 and rose vis-a-vis the euro to 4.6878/6924 from 4.7006/7058.
The ringgit was also stronger against regional peers.
It appreciated versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1556/1589 from 3.1591/1628 on Wednesday and was up against the Thai baht at 12.3331/3501 compared to 12.3688/3871 previously.
It gained vis-a-vis the Indonesian rupiah to 225.9/226.2 from 226.6/227 and remained unchanged versus the Philippine peso at 6.62/6.63.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Titijaya, Permodalan Negeri Selangor ink consortium agreement to undertake RM343mil housing project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer Titijaya Land Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, NPO Builders Sdn Bhd has entered into a consortium agreement with PNSB Construction Sdn Bhd (PCSB) to undertake an affordable housing project in Selangor.
PCSB is a wholly owned subsidiary of Permodalan Negeri Selangor Bhd (PNSB).
Titijaya said in a joint statement today that the strategic collaboration, operating under the name "Consortium of PNSB Construction-NPO" aims to undertake the development of affordable housing with a gross development value (GDV) of RM343 million under the Rumah Idaman programme.
"The consortium combines PCSB's strategic capabilities at the state level with NPO Builders' expertise in property development.
"Its primary focus is to deliver high-quality affordable homes, supporting the Selangor government's ongoing efforts and aspirations to expand homeownership opportunities among the people," it said.
Under the programme, Titijaya Land said the development will be undertaken on a strategic 5.26-hectare land parcel and will comprise 1,202 residential units.
Commenting on the agreement, Titijaya Land group managing director Datuk Lim Poh Yit said the collaboration is in line with the group's aspiration to contribute to community development through the provision of quality homes at affordable prices.
"With NPO Builders' extensive experience in project management, we are confident that this Rumah Idaman development will further strengthen access to homeownership in Selangor," he said.
Meanwhile, PNSB chief executive officer Raja Ahmad Shahrir Iskandar Raja Salim said the company is confident of optimising the implementation timeline for the Rumah Idaman project by combining PCSB's strengths in regulatory facilitation with NPO Builders' technical expertise.
"PNSB remains focused on ensuring affordability does not come at the expense of construction quality, and this consortium is founded on the principles of mutual goodwill and shared benefits," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police cripple drug syndicate in Seberang Prai, seize RM1.2mil MDMA
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police have crippled a drug syndicate following the arrest of three suspects and the seizure of MDMA worth about RM1.2 million in Seberang Prai.
The syndicate was busted in an operation by the Penang Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department between Tuesday and Wednesday.
State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said two men and a woman, aged between 24 and 33, were arrested in Seberang Prai Tengah.
He said a raid at a house in Bukit Mertajam led to the seizure of 302 plastic packets containing suspected MDMA powder weighing 8,086.80g.
He said a follow-up arrest also led to the detention of another man, believed to be the syndicate's runner operating in the same area.
"Investigations revealed the syndicate had been active since April, with the seized drugs estimated to be capable of affecting 26,956 users.
"All three suspects tested positive for ketamine in initial urine screenings.
"Police also found that two of them had prior records involving drug and criminal offences," he said in a statement.
Azizee said authorities also seized assets under the Dangerous Drugs (Forfeiture of Property) Act 1988, including two cars – a Honda City worth RM45,000 and a Kia K2 worth RM35,000.
He said other seizures included jewellery worth RM120,733.20, branded handbags valued at RM13,000 and cash amounting to RM2,050.
"The total value of seized drugs and assets amounted to RM1,426,783.20," he added.
All suspects have been remanded for seven days from yesterday until June 16 to assist investigations under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment, and whipping upon conviction.
Azizee said the operation was the result of continuous intelligence work and public cooperation, and reaffirmed police commitment to combating drug distribution in the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Job losses rise 8.4pc in May amid global supply pressures, says Akmal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Job losses in Malaysia rose by 8.4 per cent in May to 7,766 cases, amid global supply pressures linked to conflict in the Middle East, says Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
He said data from the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) showed 7,766 workers were recorded as having lost their jobs in May, compared with 7,162 in April.
"This shows an increase of 604 people, or 8.4 per cent.
"At the same time, there were positive developments in terms of job placements.
"MyFutureJobs data showed placements rose by 19 per cent, from 12,119 in April to 14,366 in May," he said during a livestreamed briefing on the energy crisis today.
Akmal said the figures covered jobseekers registered under the MyFutureJobs portal, as well as those receiving benefits under the Employment Insurance System.
He said the government would continue to monitor job losses closely while strengthening job-matching efforts to help affected workers return to employment more quickly.
He added that the labour market remained broadly stable, with the labour force increasing to 17.33 million in April, while the number of employed persons rose to 16.82 million.
The labour force participation rate stood at 70.9 per cent, reflecting continued strong engagement in the job market.
The unemployment rate remained at three per cent, involving 511,800 people.
Akmal said although there was a slight month-on-month increase, 79.5 per cent of the unemployed were still actively seeking jobs.
He said the government's priority was to safeguard jobs, ensure industries continue operating and channel assistance to those in need.
He added that the global energy crisis could not be managed in isolation, as pressure on energy supply would affect logistics, industries and prices.
The government, Akmal said, would continue using the National Economic Action Council as its main policy coordination platform to monitor data, enable early intervention and implement targeted measures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Remains of 27 Rohingya boat tragedy victims buried in Kedah
Article:
ALOR SETAR: The remains of 27 Rohingya victims who died in a boat capsize off Langkawi in November last year were laid to rest at the Old Islamic Cemetery in Tualang, Pokok Sena, today.
The victims, comprising 13 men and 14 women, were buried at 7.30am in accordance with Islamic rites under the supervision of the Kedah Islamic Religious Council (MAIK).
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the burial was carried out after the management of the remains, which began at 10pm yesterday, was completed at 7am today.
"The burial was conducted in accordance with the Temporary Controlled Burial (TCB) procedure on the advice of the hospital," he said.
On Nov 8 last year, a boat believed to be carrying about 90 undocumented migrants from Myanmar, Bangladesh and the Rohingya community reportedly broke apart at sea near the Malaysia-Thailand maritime border.
The incident in Langkawi waters involved a group of migrants believed to have been attempting to enter Malaysian waters illegally.
Following a search and rescue operation, the bodies of 27 Rohingya victims were recovered and taken to the Forensic Department of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital here.
Meanwhile, Adzli said investigations into the cause of the incident, the entry route used and the masterminds behind the migrant smuggling operation are ongoing under Section 26A of the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007 (Atipsom).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia strengthens digital education with first AI powered classroom
Article:
KANGAR: Malaysia has launched its first AI-powered classroom at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Kuala Perlis, marking a new step in the country's digital education push under the Pre-Google Reference Institution (PGRI) programme.
The initiative, part of the Pre-Google Reference Institution (PGRI) programme, is being implemented to strengthen the country's digital education transformation through an AI-powered classroom model.
Ministry of Education (MOE) deputy secretary-general (Planning and Development) Datuk Dr Megat Sany Megat Ahmad Supian said the initiative is being implemented in collaboration with various parties, including Google Malaysia.
"Perlis is the first state in Malaysia to implement the initiative involving SMK Kuala Perlis, thus paving the way for the expansion of the AI-based learning model to other schools nationwide," he said after the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail graced and officiated the launch of the AI Powered Classroom initiative at SMK Kuala Perlis.
Also in attendance was the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil.
Megat Sany said its successful implementation in Perlis would be a benchmark for the expansion of the programme to other schools in stages, with another 27 schools identified for the next phase before expanding to 800 schools over the next seven years.
He said that schools wishing to participate in the programme would need to undergo an evaluation process and meet the criteria established by Google.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hadi: Bersatu tried to weaken, undermine Pas
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has labelled his party's partnership with Bersatu as the "loosest" alliance it has ever experienced, accusing the fellow Perikatan Nasional founding member of prioritising power over Muslim unity.
He said Bersatu attempted to control the Muslim majority alongside non-Muslim parties while completely disregarding the status of Islam and the Malay-Bumiputera community.
"Bersatu tried to weaken Pas' role in pioneering political change and make a fool out of Pas by blocking the entry of more parties into the coalition," he said.
He named Malay-Muslim parties Berjasa, Pejuang, Iman and Putra, alongside non-extremist non-Muslim party MIC, as those blocked by Bersatu.
He said Bersatu acted greedily during election seat allocations by providing false information regarding its actual membership size and machinery capabilities.
"When it came to ground deployment, they were nowhere to be seen, turning Pas volunteers into forced labourers for unqualified candidates who lacked their own machinery," he said.
Hadi attributed Bersatu's severe internal crisis to a power struggle, which he said led to arbitrary sackings that bypassed moral disciplinary processes.
He said this internal turmoil resulted in Bersatu sidelining, suspending and sacking a significant number of its own members of parliament, including those who had been active alongside Pas.
"When added together, the number amounts to two-thirds of the party's members, which could undermine PN's strength and damage its dignity," he said.
He highlighted instances of what he termed a lack of loyalty among allies, citing political manoeuvres in Perlis that led to the downfall of the Pas-led state's menteri besar.
He also accused Bersatu of disrupting planned political operations out of a desire for the prime minister's post, alleging it botched an operation to take over DAP-influenced Negri Sembilan by causing the withdrawal of statutory declarations of support despite securing a sufficient majority.
"Driven by the need to protect Pas' principled struggle against these opportunistic moves, the party is now compelled to take a firm stance," he said.
He said Pas had attempted to address these flaws through meetings and private discussions, but Bersatu remained too preoccupied with its internal woes to act.
"In the end, Pas had to swallow a bitter pill during the general election, by-elections and state elections," he said.
Moving forward, Hadi said Pas would no longer feel bound by the structural rules of the alliance.
He said the Pas syura council, alongside scholars and political analysts, has been tasked with conducting a thorough review of the alliance based on syariah law and political interest.
Pas on June 8 formally terminated its
political cooperation
with Bersatu following a special central working committee meeting at the party's Kuala Lumpur headquarters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 11, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended slightly higher today, with the FBM KLCI staying in positive territory in the afternoon despite mixed regional markets.
Gains were driven by selective buying in petrochemical, utilities and consumer stocks, while profit-taking in the same sectors limited further upside.
The market stayed resilient as investors continued to favour defensive, domestically focused stocks amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties and a cautious external outlook.
Meanwhile, the ringgit fell against the US dollar to 4.670.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin rose to RM255,377.
Ethereum rose to RM6,735, while Solana was up at RM265.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Home Ministry rolls out refugee registration, status determination process
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry, through the Immigration Department, is implementing a Refugee Registration and Status Determination process in Peninsular Malaysia.
In a statement today, the ministry said the move was crucial to safeguard internal security and improve management of the country's migration affairs.
It said that while Malaysia had seen a growing presence of refugees and United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders for decades, comprehensive data on identities and backgrounds remained incomplete.
"To date, the government does not possess complete and comprehensive data on the actual number, identities and backgrounds of those residing in the country under these categories," it said in a statement.
The process will involve UNHCR cardholders and other individuals claiming refugee status.
"Through it, the government will obtain biometric data and more detailed identity information to enable the status, whereabouts and backgrounds of the individuals concerned to be identified more accurately," it said.
The ministry said the process would help assess security, health and economic implications using verifiable data, while also strengthening migration policies and enforcement related to refugees and UNHCR cardholders.
However, it said that the process was not a pathway to citizenship, permanent residency or the right to reside permanently in Malaysia.
"Rather, it is an important instrument to ensure that the government has better control over migration issues and national security.
"The government's principle is clear. National security will never be compromised," it said.
It added that the initiative was a step towards a more organised and responsible migration management system grounded in national security and public interest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: State govts urged to build innovation ecosystems to boost start-ups
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Science, Technology and Innovation Ministry (Mosti) has proposed that all state governments establish their own innovation ecosystems to accelerate the development of start-ups nationwide.
Its minister Chang Lih Kang said federal agencies were ready to extend cooperation and support should state governments pursue the initiative.
He said the collaboration between the Selangor Information Technology and Digital Economy Corporation (Sidec) and the Malaysian Research Accelerator for Technology and Innovation (MRANTI) under the Selangor Triple Accelerator 2026 programme was a strong example of linking state initiatives with national platforms.
"The collaboration between state and federal government agencies allows us to develop a more vibrant start-up ecosystem. So, I encourage all states to do the same," he told reporters after launching the programme here today.
Earlier in his speech, Chang said the programme offered three key pathways for entrepreneurs and innovators – DeepX, focusing on deep technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), semiconductors and biotechnology; RetailX, supporting businesses leveraging digital and omnichannel platforms; and TokenX, catering to enterprises operating in digital assets.
"The partnership between Sidec and MRANTI brings together strong market access and world-class innovation infrastructure.
"Together, they can help nurture a new generation of Malaysian companies that are competitive not only domestically but also on the global stage," he said.
He added that Mosti remained committed to supporting such efforts through access to research and development (R&D) and commercialisation funding, talent development and regulatory facilitation to enable enterprises to scale regionally and globally.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL, agencies join forces against unlicensed foreign traders
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: City Hall (DBKL) will establish a multi-agency task force to address the growing presence of foreign traders in the city.
Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said a decision to step up enforcement against foreign traders and hawkers in the city was made at today's Special Task Force on Agency Reform (Star) meeting.
The meeting was also attended by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and Selayang Municipal Council (MPS) president Shahman Jalaludin.
Speaking after the announcement of  Backlane Upgrading Works at Desa Sri Hartamas today, Fadlun said DBKL had conducted more than 4,000 enforcement operations targeting business activities at markets across the city.
"We've conducted operations in wholesale markets, Pudu, and so on. It might seem like nothing is happening, but our efforts are continuous," he said.
Meanwhile, he noted the importance of mobilising efforts with other agencies to improve the enforcement operations, given that DBKL is not permitted to make arrests of individuals and is only limited to addressing licensing matters.
This includes the participation of the immigration department and the Social Welfare Department through the formation of a task force which will be officially announced by the government later.
Speaking at the same event,  Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said enforcement operations must be carried out with other agencies.
"We have requested that more integrated enforcement operations be conducted to ensure that these actions are effective," Yeoh said.
Recently, there has been a significant increase in the number of unlicensed foreign traders in Selayang. This has raised concerns among local businesses, who complain they are being
undercut by cheaper goods
from foreign counterparts operating without valid trading permits.
Previous reports also cited views from the public on the inconsistency of enforcement actions towards foreign traders.
Separately, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay also noted the
need for a multi agency collaboration
to strengthen enforcement efforts to overcome the Rohingya refugee issue in Malaysia.
Speaking at a Police Day community outreach programme at the Tuanku Ampuan Besar Tuanku Aishah Rohani Hospital today, he assured the public that local authorities are proactively conducting operations against illegal immigrants and unlawful settlements, rather than acting only when issues attract public attention.
On June 5, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had instructed authorities to take action against any parties
breaching or misusing business licenses
.
Though the concerns raised were not targeted towards any specific groups, the government noted the increasing complaints over the takeover of local business activities by foreigners – prompting authorities to strengthen enforcement efforts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt braces for El Niño as energy crisis risks deepen
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is preparing for the possible impact of El Niño, which could intensify pressure on food, water and energy supplies amid the ongoing global energy crisis triggered by tensions in the Middle East.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the phenomenon, which had been brought to the attention of the National Economic Action Council (NEAC), was expected to begin this month and could continue until at least the middle of next year.
He said El Niño could bring hotter and drier conditions, with rainfall in several states potentially declining by between 40 and 60 per cent.
"The government is also giving attention, apart from geopolitical issues, to the risk of extreme weather, including the El Niño phenomenon, which is expected to begin in June and could continue at least until the middle of 2027.
"This phenomenon could also add pressure to the ongoing energy crisis, and the matter has been brought to the attention of NEAC," he said during a live-streamed briefing on the energy crisis today.
Akmal said the risk should not be underestimated as it could affect water supply, increase the likelihood of haze, place pressure on public health, disrupt food crops and commodities, and drive up energy demand.
He cited the 2015-2016 El Niño episode, when temperatures exceeding 37°C led to the closure of 250 schools in the northern states of Peninsular Malaysia.
The palm oil industry also recorded a decline in production of between 16 and 18 per cent during that period, he said.
"Major commodity sectors will be significantly affected when El Niño occurs, including a possible reduction in crop yields by an average of eight to 10 per cent overall."
Akmal said the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) had activated early preparedness measures, including readiness to carry out cloud-seeding operations should conditions worsen.
He said NEAC had also decided that the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability would coordinate engagements with state governments to brief them on El Niño risks and facilitate early preparations.
Akmal said the government would continue using NEAC as its main policy coordination platform to monitor developments, act early and implement targeted interventions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Disciplinary hair check led to confrontation at school – Perak Education Dept
Article:
IPOH: The state Education Department has clarified that an incident involving a male discipline teacher and several students at a secondary school in Kuala Kangsar was triggered by provocation during a hair inspection exercise.
Its director Zulkafli Mohamed Mokhtar said the incident at 7.50am yesterday occurred during a disciplinary check on students' appearance at the school's multipurpose hall.
He said three Form Five students had refused to comply with instructions from the head of discipline to sit at a designated area during the inspection.
"During the inspection, students with styled or dyed hair were instructed to sit at the staircase area of the hall.
"However, three students refused to comply and responded in a rude tone," he said when contacted today.
Zulkafli said the situation escalated when the teacher lost his temper and acted out of character towards one of the students involved.
He added that the teacher was later calmed down, while two other students fled the scene shouting and left the school compound.
Following the incident, he said the school administration held a meeting with the students' parents in an effort to resolve the matter.
"A further meeting will be held involving the Kuala Kangsar education office, the school liaison officer and representatives from the Parents and Teachers Association," he said.
He said parents had agreed that students must comply with school regulations, particularly on hair appearance, and expressed hope that disciplinary matters would be handled appropriately.
Earlier, Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairuddin Abu Hanipah said he was awaiting a detailed report from the Perak Education Department before issuing any statement.
Initial reports had suggested that a
discipline teacher
was injured after allegedly being punched by a group of students following an altercation over hair regulations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pro-Net unveils upgraded e.Mas 7 Premium Plus
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PROTON Holdings Bhd's electric vehicle unit Pro-Net today unveiled the Proton e.Mas 7 Premium Plus, an upgraded variant of Malaysia's first national EV priced from RM125,800.
The variant offers a larger battery, extended driving range and enhanced comfort features.
The eMas 7 Premium Plus is equipped with a 68.39kWh Aegis short blade battery, delivering an estimated driving range of up to 450km under the WLTP, making it suitable for both daily commuting and longer-distance travel.
Pro-Net chief executive officer Zhang Qiang said the launch marks the next phase in the evolution of the e.Mas brand, which has grown to become Malaysia's leading EV brand.
"By combining greater driving range with enhanced comfort, the Premium Plus makes EV ownership even more practical and enjoyable.
"It further strengthens the Proton e.Mas 7's appeal as a family-ready EV for both daily commutes and long-distance journeys," he said at the launch in conjunction with the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show (KLIMS) 2026 today.
The e.Mas 7 lineup also includes the Premium and Prime variants, which are priced at RM119,800 and RM103,800 respectively in Peninsular Malaysia.
Zhang said the extended range is designed to provide greater flexibility for both urban and intercity driving while helping to address range anxiety, one of the key considerations among EV buyers.
To enhance cabin comfort, the Premium Plus introduces several new features, including dual front massage seats, a powered front passenger seat with four-way adjustment and memory function, as well as an integrated passenger leg rest.
Despite its larger battery capacity, the vehicle retains DC fast-charging capability of up to 115 kW, enabling charging from 30 per cent to 80 per cent in about 20 minutes, similar to the existing Premium variant.
Pro-Net also introduced the power pack plus package for customers purchasing the new variant.
The package includes a complimentary trade-in processing fee, an additional RM5,000 trade-in top-up for existing Proton owners, a free seven kilowatt charging wallbox, a vehicle-to-load (V2L) converter, five years of unlimited internet data, as well as vehicle and battery warranty coverage.
For a limited period, customers purchasing any e.Mas 7 variant will also enjoy a special launch rebate of RM7,000.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: In ageing South Korea, AI dolls care for the elderly
Article:
IN her tiny apartment in South Korea, where she lives alone, 78-year-old Bang Chun-ja spends her days with a childlike AI-powered doll she says she prefers to people.
The doll greets Bang when she returns home, sings to her when she feels bored, reminds her not to skip meals or medication – helping her maintain a routine – and tells her it loves her.
Bang has limited contact with her grown-up daughter, and fell into severe depression after major back surgery, spending hours alone staring at the ceiling in pain.
After a difficult divorce and years of hard work as a hairdresser and single mother, Bang told AFP that "at this age, there is nothing harder than being hurt by people."
But "when I'm with Hyodol, I never get hurt", she said, holding the cuddly doll with pigtails and a pink gingham dress, provided by her local municipality.
The doll "only makes me laugh", she added.
Bang is one of many South Koreans battling loneliness in a country where birth rates are among the world's lowest and almost half the population is 50 or older.
In 2024, South Korea recorded more than 3,920 "lonely deaths", people dying alone and remaining undiscovered for extended periods – the highest since records began in 2017.
Around 42 per cent of households in the Asian tech powerhouse are single-person, with social isolation affecting the country's vulnerable elderly in particular.
Authorities are providing AI care devices – some designed to detect signs of lonely deaths – to seniors living alone, including in districts of Seoul and in Yongin, south of the capital.
They also include a smiling robot made by the company Wonderful Platform, and similar cute dolls from the firm Mr. Mind.
In the United States, a lamp-like AI device called ElliQ offers similar companionship and safety-monitoring services.
Hyodol, the startup behind the dolls of the same name, says around 14,500 are in use in South Korea, whether owned by individuals, rented out by governments or used in nursing homes.
Bang, in Yongin, said her daughter lives far away and has health issues of her own, so "having Hyodol by my side is a huge help."
[[nid:1460813]]
Developing the doll involved years of field research, said company head Kim Ji-hee.
Hyodol can converse using ChatGPT, but is also programmed with scripts based on Kim's real-life interviews.
People she met included a widow estranged from her adult children over financial woes, who stored piles of their belongings at their mother's home.
"She lived alone with four fridges and three washing machines," Kim, 49, told AFP.
The interviews revealed the "pain of having no one to tell when something upsetting happens, and no one to share with when something joyful happens", Kim said.
Hyodol has strict data security protocols, with voice recordings only used internally to train the doll's chatbot, Kim said.
Users give prior consent for certain health-related recordings, such as those related to sleep, mood, meals and pain levels, to be shared with their welfare workers.
Because many interviewees spoke fondly of parental or mentor figures, Hyodol was created as a grandchild-like companion designed to "love its users unconditionally," Kim said.
One of the earliest scripts she wrote was a greeting for users returning home, which she wanted to feel like the "warmest welcome in the whole wide world."
"Grandma, where have you been? I waited for you all day," it says. "Next time you go out, please take me with you!"
[[nid:1460812]]
Made with soft, cushiony materials, the doll also makes spontaneous requests, asking users to pat its head, hold its hand or share snacks with it, although it cannot eat.
Hyodol's character was designed to be dependent on its user, as many older Koreans have spent their lives enduring gruelling work to provide for their families, Kim said.
"When they begin to feel they are no longer needed, they experience a profound sense of emptiness."
Oh Sun-hwa, a nurse who recommended the doll to Bang, said she had seen it significantly ease depression among seniors living alone.
But she also worried the technology could further reduce human contact, with family members potentially visiting less if they felt AI devices were caring for their parents.
In her apartment, another user, 79-year-old Kim Young-bun, said the doll was a source of comfort.
"I had no one to talk to all day – to the point my mouth almost felt stale from not speaking. But then this little one came along and chatters with me all the time."
"I'm so grateful to be with you again today," the doll told Kim in a chirpy cartoon-like voice.
"So am I," Kim replied, doting on the doll.
"Thanks for being with me. I love you," the device said.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perak plans RM38.7mil observatory to boost astronomy, moon-sighting research
Article:
IPOH: The Perak government is planning to develop a RM38.65 million state observatory in Manjung to strengthen falak (Islamic astronomy) activities and expand astronomy education and research.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the proposed Perak Observatory would be built at Pantai Pasir Panjang, Segari, near the existing Baitul Hilal observation point, which has long been used for official crescent moon sighting activities in the state.
He said the proposal followed an intensive workshop on the development of the observatory held on May 19 and 20, involving the Perak State Mufti Department, the Public Works Department (PWD), the State Finance Office and the State Economic Planning Unit (UPEN).
"To strengthen existing facilities and expand falak and astronomy activities, the state government is planning the development of the Perak State Observatory," he said after launching Perak Astronomy Month at a hotel here today.
Saarani said the proposed observatory would occupy a 3.8-hectare site at Pantai Pasir Panjang and serve as the state's official falak observation centre.
"It is not only for moon-sighting activities, but also to expand the study of falak and astronomy," he said.
He added that the project has the potential to become a new educational tourism and astronomy attraction for the state.
"The estimated cost of the project is RM38.65 million, subject to further technical studies and government approval," he said.
When asked who would bear the cost, Saarani said the state government would fund the project.
On the construction timeline, he said it would depend on the PWD, which has been tasked with overseeing the project, including the appointment of consultants and finalising the design.
He added that the existing Baitul Hilal facility would continue to be used for crescent moon sighting activities, as the new observatory is aimed at expanding falak and astronomy studies beyond that purpose.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: FMM calls for timely intervention to avoid long-term manufacturing risks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Manufacturers require timely and targeted intervention to prevent temporary disruptions from becoming longer-term structural disadvantages for Malaysian manufacturers, according to the Federation of Malaysian Manufacturers (FMM).
Its president Jacob Lee Chor Kok said that while many companies have demonstrated resilience through inventory management, supplier diversification and operational adjustments, these measures are increasingly reaching their limits as the crisis enters a prolonged phase.
"Manufacturing remains a key pillar of the Malaysian economy, contributing significantly to exports, employment, investment and industrial development.
"Sustaining the sector's competitiveness during this period is therefore a national economic priority.
"Measures that help manufacturers manage rising costs, secure critical inputs, maintain liquidity and preserve market competitiveness will be essential to protecting industrial capacity and investor confidence," he said in a statement today.
Lee was commenting on FMM's third survey on the impact of the Middle East conflict on Malaysian manufacturing, trade and supply chains.
The survey drew responses from 196 companies across a broad cross-section of industries between May 25 and June 10, 2026.
It is the third consecutive monthly assessment since disruptions first emerged in February this year.
According to Lee, the three surveys trace a clear progression, with the first capturing the freight and energy cost shock, and the second showing its spread into raw materials, cash flow and investment.
He added that the third survey shows these pressures persisting alongside a more serious development, where manufacturers are now losing orders, abandoning product lines and losing customers.
"The prolonged crisis has moved beyond a cost problem and is inflicting lasting commercial damage," he said.
According to the survey findings, operating conditions remain under severe pressure across the manufacturing sector, despite various mitigation measures.
Cost pressures have turned into commercial losses, with the most serious finding being that these costs are no longer being absorbed as a passing challenge but have become real commercial losses.
Freight and logistics remain the primary constraint, with freight costs continuing to be the dominant pressure.
About 54 per cent reported further increases since early May and 86 per cent remain at or above peak levels recorded in the second survey, with no commercial substitute for sea freight for most manufacturers.
Investment and employment decisions are being affected as the prolonged disruption is now shaping investment and workforce decisions.
Delays in industrial investment and technology upgrading carry lasting risks for Malaysia's productivity, industrial transformation agenda and future competitiveness.
The survey also examined domestic sourcing for the first time, with 46 per cent of companies having sourced or attempted to source from Malaysian suppliers.
However, 36 per cent reported that required specifications or grades were unavailable locally, while 31 per cent found local pricing uncompetitive against imports, indicating that targeted measures are needed to address capacity and competitiveness gaps.
FMM also welcomed government support, acknowledging the government's response following the second survey presentation to the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) on May 19.
However, the survey findings indicate that relief measures have yet to reach a significant proportion of affected manufacturers.
The findings point to a financing gap affecting a segment of manufacturers that falls outside existing support frameworks, despite continuing cost and cash flow pressures.
Lee said the pattern across the three surveys shows a disruption that is becoming structural, and without targeted intervention the cumulative impact on costs, competitiveness, investment and employment will intensify.
On this basis, Lee said FMM urges the government to consider six measures, including duty and tax relief for alternative-origin raw materials, as well as a double tax deduction for crisis-related freight and insurance costs.
He also called for industrial fuel cost relief, dedicated financing for mid-tier manufacturers, deferral of remaining port tariff increases, and government-facilitated domestic supplier matching.
Lee said FMM will submit the findings of the third survey to the relevant authorities and continue to engage stakeholders as the situation evolves.
He added that the survey points to a narrowing window to prevent current disruptions from translating into longer-term losses in competitiveness, market share and industrial capacity.
"Timely and targeted intervention will be essential to protect jobs, sustain investor confidence and ensure that Malaysian manufacturers emerge from this prolonged crisis in a position to recover and compete effectively when global supply chain conditions stabilise," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Economy minister warns El Nino could cut rainfall by up to 60pc and impact economy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Malaysia is preparing for the possible return of the El Niño weather phenomenon, which is expected to begin in June 2026 and potentially last until at least mid-2027.
Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the phenomenon could bring hotter weather and a sharp reduction in rainfall of between 40 and 60 per cent in several states.
“This risk must not be underestimated as it may affect water supply, increase haze risk, place pressure on public health, impact food and commodity crops, increase energy demand, and affect the overall macroeconomic environment,” he said during an online global supply crisis briefing today.
He said past El Niño events had shown how severe the consequences could be for both the economy and public services.
In 2015 and 2016, he said high temperatures exceeding 37 degrees Celsius led to the closure of 250 schools in northern Peninsular Malaysia.
He added that the palm oil industry recorded a production decline of around 16 to 18 per cent during that period.
“Overall, key commodity sectors may experience yield reductions of around 8 to 10 per cent during an El Niño event,” he said.
On Tuesday, National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim said the government is stepping up national preparedness measures in response to forecasts of hotter and drier conditions linked to the El Niño phenomenon.
He said that although the situation remains under control, the government is treating the outlook seriously and has directed all relevant agencies to enhance readiness and implement early mitigation measures.
As part of these early intervention efforts, three cloud-seeding operations have been planned in the near future to help improve rainfall distribution and support reservoir levels in affected areas.

Title: Economy minister: RM200 advance paddy incentive reaches nearly 180,000 farmers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government has introduced a one-off advance ploughing incentive of RM200 per hectare for paddy farmers in Peninsular Malaysia to support preparations for the first paddy planting season of 2026.
He said the payments, which began in phases on May 19, are aimed at reducing farmers’ initial capital burden and accelerating early planting preparations.
“To date, 90 per cent, or nearly 180,000 farmers, have received the payment,” he said during an online global supply crisis briefing today.
Reflecting on the past 100 days since the onset of the crisis in West Asia, he said Malaysia has managed global supply disruptions through a data-driven approach, cross-ministerial coordination and targeted interventions.
He said a total of 113 high-frequency indicators have been developed to support faster risk assessments and are now displayed on a global supply crisis monitoring dashboard covering economic, energy, food, commodity and logistics indicators.
In addition, he said food price monitoring continues through the PriceCatcher system, with the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) tracking 316 essential items across more than 2,000 retail premises nationwide.
To date, he said 28 policy papers across 13 economic sectors have been discussed under the M10 mechanism.
“From these discussions, 126 decisions have been recorded, of which 15 have been fully implemented while 111 remain under active implementation as medium- and long-term measures.
“This demonstrates that M10 is not merely a discussion forum but a platform for action, where every decision is translated into implementation at the ministry and agency levels,” he added.

Title: Malaysia’s medicine and medical device supply stays stable amid global pressures, says economy minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the supply of medicines and medical devices in Malaysia remains stable, with most items classified as low risk.
He said the government will continue strengthening buffer stocks and expanding supply sources when necessary.
“The supply of medicines and medical devices remains generally stable.
“Based on monitoring by the Health Ministry up to May 8, 2026, 72 per cent of medicine items are at low-risk levels, while for medical devices, 81.7 per cent are at low-risk levels,” he said during an online global supply crisis briefing today.
Among recent initiatives, he said the Health Ministry, through the Medical Device Authority, signed a memorandum of cooperation (MoC) with Japan’s Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare on June 8, 2026, in conjunction with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s official visit to Japan.
The announcement comes amid a broader global supply crisis that has placed pressure on healthcare supply chains, with disruptions affecting the availability and distribution of medicines and medical devices in several regions.
Factors such as geopolitical tensions, shipping bottlenecks and fluctuating production costs have heightened the need for countries to strengthen resilience in critical medical supplies.

Title: KPDN seizes 6,000 litres of diesel in Melaka raid under Op Tiris 4.0 over suspected subsidy diversion
Article:
MELAKA, June 11 — The Melaka Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 6,000 litres of diesel believed to have been diverted in an Integrated Op Tiris 4.0 raid on a premises in Taman IKS Paya Rumput here last night.
Its director Mohd Hazimin Jamaludin said also seized in the raid at 9 pm last night were a lorry, an additional tank with a capacity of 3,000 litres, two skid tanks, two fuel cards, several fuel pumps and a flow meter estimated to be worth a total of RM106,345.
He said all the equipment and vehicles were believed to be used for illegal storage and sale of diesel at the unnamed premises.
“In addition, a local man and a Bangladeshi man, both in their 40s, who were in the premises were also detained to assist in the investigation.
“The raid was carried out based on intelligence and planned monitoring in an effort to curb diesel diversion activities in Melaka, thus ensuring that the leakage of state subsidies can be completely contained,” he said in a statement here today.
He said the case was being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961 for violating Subregulation 21(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974, namely carrying out wholesale, retail, or manufacturing transactions of any scheduled goods (diesel) without a valid license from the director of Supply Control.
In the meantime, he said individuals or companies are reminded not to manipulate, divert or deal with controlled goods without a valid permit and license.
Mohd Hazimin said strict action without compromise would be taken against any party found to be violating the law, especially involving the diversion of subsidised goods.
“The Melaka KPDN will continue to intensify its commitment in combating the leakage of controlled goods through firm and continuous enforcement actions to protect the interests of consumers and the country’s economic stability.
“Consumers are also asked to be the eyes and ears of KPDN in reporting misconduct related to trade activities and consumer issues through various complaint channels including WhatsApp at 019-848 8000 and the e-aduan@kpdn.gov.my portal,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Remains of 27 Rohingya migrants who died in boat capsize laid to rest in Kedah
Article:
ALOR SETAR, June 11 — The remains of 27 Rohingya victims from Myanmar who died in a boat capsize incident last year were laid to rest at the Kampung Tualang Islamic Cemetery near Pokok Sena today.
Kedah Police Chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the remains of 13 men and 14 women who died in the tragic incident was recovered in Langkawi waters last year.
“The unfortunate incident involved a group of migrants attempting to enter the country’s waters illegally. All recovered bodies were sent to the Forensic Medicine Unit of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital for identity verification, post-mortems, and forensic documentation.
“The management of the remains began at 10 pm yesterday and concluded at 7 am today. All the bodies were safely buried starting from 7:30 am with full decorum and respect in accordance with Islamic rites, under the supervision of the Kedah Islamic Religious Council (MAIK),” he said in a statement today.
He added that the burial arrangements were carried out through an integrated effort with several agencies, including the police, the hospital, MAIK, the Kedah Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAIK), the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM), the Public Works Department (JKR), and the Department of Survey and Mapping Malaysia (JUPEM).
“Kedah Police express their appreciation and gratitude to all government agencies and involved parties who demonstrated a high level of cooperation and commitment in ensuring everything was handled properly.
“The investigation into the cause of the incident, the entry route, and the masterminds behind this migrant smuggling activity is ongoing under Section 26A of the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007 (ATIPSOM),” he said.
Adzli further urged members of the public with any information to come forward to the nearest police station to assist with the investigation.
“The incident is a deeply heartbreaking tragedy and serves as a reminder of the risks and dangers faced by individuals involved in migrant smuggling activities through un-gazetted routes.
“The police will continue to strengthen law enforcement and security controls over national waters to ensure that the country’s safety and sovereignty are always preserved,” he said.
Last year, media outlets reported that approximately 290 to 300 undocumented migrants had boarded a vessel from Myanmar and were later transferred to a smaller boat upon reaching the Malaysia-Thailand border waters before the smaller boat capsized. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia, Turkmenistan to seal hydrocarbons partnership on June 19 as energy security push intensifies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Malaysia and Turkmenistan are expected to sign a long-term hydrocarbons cooperation agreement on June 19 as part of efforts to strengthen the country’s energy supply chain, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
He said the government is actively enhancing bilateral and multilateral cooperation, including recent positive developments in energy collaboration between Petronas and Japanese partner JERA Co., Inc., involving the supply of liquefied natural gas (LNG), formalised through a long-term agreement that is expected to benefit both countries.
“This also includes cooperation through the Asia Zero Emission Community (AZEC), Partnership on Wide Energy and Resources Resilience Asia (POWERR Asia), energy cooperation with Australia, as well as a long-term hydrocarbons partnership with Turkmenistan, which is expected to be signed on June 19, 2026,” said Akmal Nasrullah during a briefing on the global supply crisis streamed on the ministry’s Facebook page today.
He said the crisis has tested Malaysia’s economic resilience but at the same time created opportunities for the country to strengthen its long-term resilience.
“With a strong economic foundation, a strategic energy position and data-driven economic management, Malaysia is not only capable of managing global uncertainties but is also well-positioned to move forward with greater confidence,” said Akmal Nasrullah.
He also said the average LNG price rose moderately by 4.6 per cent to US$18.87 per million British thermal units (MMBtu) during the period from June 2 to 6, 2026, from US$18.04 previously.
The movement remained within a manageable range, indicating that the global LNG market remained stable despite geopolitical uncertainties.
